chunk_summary: Therapeutic iron treatment for iron deficiency anemia involves specific dosing, monitoring for response, and addressing non-response through adherence, ferritin interpretation, and screening for underlying conditions.
learning_objectives: Understand therapeutic iron dosing guidelines for mild, moderate, and severe anemia.
Learn strategies to monitor and evaluate non-response to iron therapy.
Identify potential causes of inadequate response to iron treatment and appropriate follow-up actions.
key_facts: Therapeutic dosing for mild to moderate anemia: ~3 mg/kg/day elemental iron; severe anemia: up to ~6 mg/kg/day under supervision.
Continue therapy for at least 3 months, and 2–3 months after hemoglobin normalization to replete iron stores.
Recheck hemoglobin after 2–4 weeks to monitor response; expected improvement supports adherence and diagnosis.
Avoid co-administration of iron with milk or tea; pair with vitamin C to enhance absorption.
Evaluate non-response by reassessing adherence, dosing, blood loss, ferritin with CRP/ESR, and hemoglobinopathies.
reference_quote: Daily iron supplementation in infants and children is recommended to prevent and treat iron deficiency anemia. (Source: Nutritional anemia recommendations, IAP joint committee)
document_key_topics: Monitoring and managing non-response to iron therapy
Addressing underlying causes of persistent anemia
